hero.headline: UPES Online MBA in Operations Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: University of Petroleum and Energy Studies (UPES) Online MBA with an Operations Management specialisation. NIRF #45 University, NIRF #36 Management, NAAC A, UGC-DEB approved. Production Planning and Inventory Control, Product Lifecycle Management, Lean Manufacturing, Logistics and SCM, Service Operations, Technology Management, TQM, and Innovation in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: UPES Online MBA Operations Management: NIRF #45 University, NIRF #36 Management, NAAC A, fees Rs 1,75,000 (Year 1: Rs 95,000 / Year 2: Rs 80,000), zero-cost EMI at Rs 7,292/month. Operations depth with Production Planning, PLM, Lean Manufacturing, Logistics, Service Operations, Technology Management, TQM, and Innovation and Entrepreneurship.

about.body:
The UPES Online MBA in Operations Management is offered by University of Petroleum and Energy Studies, Dehradun. UPES holds NAAC A accreditation, NIRF #45 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #36 in Management (2025). The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The Operations Management specialisation delivers Sem 3-4 subjects across the full operations management stack. Sem 3 covers Production Planning and Inventory Control, Product Lifecycle Management, Lean Manufacturing, Introduction to Logistics and SCM, and Service Operations. Sem 4 covers Strategic Management, Technology Management, Total Quality Management, Innovation and Entrepreneurship, and Dissertation.

Product Lifecycle Management as a dedicated operations MBA subject reflects UPES's engineering and technology heritage. Combined with Lean Manufacturing and TQM, this specialisation is particularly well-suited for professionals at automotive, manufacturing, oil and gas, and infrastructure companies - the sectors where UPES alumni networks are most active. Technology Management in Sem 4 also positions graduates for operations roles at IT and tech manufacturing firms.

sem3Subjects.name: Production Planning and Inventory Control
sem3Subjects.description: Apply MRP, capacity requirements planning, and inventory optimisation models to manufacturing and warehouse operations management.

sem3Subjects.name: Product Lifecycle Management
sem3Subjects.description: Manage product development stages, PLM software tools, and cross-functional design-to-delivery operations for manufacturing roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Lean Manufacturing
sem3Subjects.description: Apply 5S, Kaizen, value stream mapping, and Just-in-Time principles to factory floor and process improvement management.

sem3Subjects.name: Intro to Logistics and SCM
sem3Subjects.description: Analyse end-to-end supply chain networks, manage third-party logistics, and optimise last-mile delivery and warehousing operations.

sem3Subjects.name: Service Operations
sem3Subjects.description: Apply service quality frameworks, manage service capacity and queuing, and design customer-facing service delivery systems.

sem4Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem4Subjects.description: Develop operations-led competitive strategy, evaluate make-or-buy decisions, and design supply chain-led business growth plans.

sem4Subjects.name: Technology Management
sem4Subjects.description: Evaluate technology adoption decisions, manage digital operations transformation, and apply Industry 4.0 frameworks to manufacturing.

sem4Subjects.name: Total Quality Management
sem4Subjects.description: Apply TQM frameworks, Six Sigma DMAIC methodology, ISO quality standards, and process control tools for quality management roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Innovation and Entrepreneurship
sem4Subjects.description: Apply innovation frameworks to operations product design and build entrepreneurial operations management approaches.

sem4Subjects.name: Dissertation
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied operations research project on lean transformation, SCM optimisation, or technology-led operations improvement.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all UPES MBA specialisations. Core includes Managerial Economics, Accounting for Managers, Marketing Management, Quantitative Techniques, Operations Management, OB and HRM, Project Management, Financial Management, Research Methodology, and Business Analytics for Managers.

whoHires.body:
Operations Management MBA graduates from UPES Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Manufacturing, Automotive, Energy and petroleum, FMCG operations, E-commerce logistics, Infrastructure and construction

**Entry-level roles:** Operations Executive, Production Planner, Quality Analyst, SCM Analyst, Process Improvement Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Operations Manager, Production Manager, Supply Chain Manager, Quality Manager, Plant Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Head of Operations, VP Manufacturing, COO, Director Supply Chain, VP Quality

**Top hiring organisations (UPES alumni placements):** TCS, Infosys, HCL, Wipro, Deloitte, KPMG, and manufacturing and energy sector companies. UPES's strong industry connect with oil and gas, power, automotive, and infrastructure sectors creates direct operations career pathways at companies like ONGC, NTPC, Maruti Suzuki, and Tata Motors that recruit UPES operations graduates specifically.

skills.body:
The UPES Operations Management specialisation builds skills that manufacturing, energy, and logistics employers test at operations manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Production Planning and Inventory Control: MRP and capacity planning, inventory optimisation modelling, and production scheduling for manufacturing operations manager roles
- Lean Manufacturing: 5S, Kaizen, value stream mapping, and JIT application for factory floor improvement and operations efficiency roles
- Total Quality Management: TQM framework deployment, Six Sigma DMAIC, ISO quality standard management, and process control for quality management roles
- Product Lifecycle Management: PLM software management and cross-functional design-to-delivery operations for manufacturing firms with complex product portfolios

**Applied skills:**
- Technology Management: Industry 4.0 technology adoption evaluation, digital operations transformation, and smart manufacturing systems management
- Introduction to Logistics and SCM: supply chain network design, 3PL management, and logistics analytics for operations and supply chain manager roles

**Soft skills:** Presenting lean transformation results to plant and operations leadership, managing cross-functional production and quality teams, communicating technology adoption proposals to manufacturing boards, and leading TQM culture programmes across factory operations.

comparisons.body:
Comparing UPES Online MBA Operations Management against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Operations Management:** Amity Operations at Rs 2,25,000 covers operations strategy and supply chain broadly. UPES Operations Management at Rs 1,75,000 adds Product Lifecycle Management and Technology Management as dedicated subjects not in most Amity operations programmes. UPES's NIRF #36 Management ranking is stronger than Amity's management category performance. For energy and manufacturing sector operations careers, UPES alumni networks give direct access.

**vs LPU Online MBA Operations Management:** LPU Operations covers logistics, SCM, and operations with NIRF #31 University ranking at Rs 1,61,600 with grant. UPES Operations Management includes PLM and Technology Management as distinct subjects. UPES's NIRF #36 Management ranking is stronger than LPU's management category standing. For automotive, energy, and infrastructure operations, UPES carries stronger sector-specific industry connect.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Operations and Data Sciences Management:** NMIMS Operations combines quantitative analytics with operations management and carries strong Mumbai brand. For Mumbai FMCG and pharma operations, NMIMS is the stronger choice. For North India manufacturing, energy sector operations, and supply chain roles where UPES alumni are active, UPES Operations Management is the better-positioned option.

fees.body:
The UPES Online MBA in Operations Management has a total program fee of Rs 1,75,000 across 2 years (4 semesters).

**Year-wise fee schedule:**
- Year 1 (Semesters 1 and 2): Rs 95,000
- Year 2 (Semesters 3 and 4): Rs 80,000

**Zero-cost EMI (no-cost EMI available):**
- Rs 7,292 per month for 24 months
- Tenure options: 3 to 36 months
- Zero interest (0% cost EMI) through UPES NBFC lending tie-ups
- Contact our counsellor for current tenure options and approval terms

**Scholarships available (confirmed from UPES scholarships portal):**
- **Academic/Work Experience scholarship**: 10% for 3+ years experience with 55%+ graduation marks; 15% for 5+ years experience; 20% for 10+ years experience
- **Alumni scholarship**: 20% for UPES degree programme graduates
- **Vijay (Sports) scholarship**: 35% to 100% based on national/international achievement level
- Scholarships cannot be combined with corporate MOU discounts
- Scholarship benefit applied semester-wise; eligibility confirmed at admission

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with UPES each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees, scholarship eligibility, and EMI terms with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by University of Petroleum and Energy Studies (UPES) and carries the UPES name with the MBA designation. The Operations Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India. UPES is WES recognised, making the degree suitable for international credential evaluation.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from UPES Online MBA Operations Management alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Suresh Bisht
items.liked: Lean Manufacturing and TQM were directly applicable to my manufacturing plant role. Technology Management subject gave Industry 4.0 frameworks I presented to our plant head. PLM subject was unique.
items.disliked: Six Sigma content in TQM could be more hands-on with Green Belt level practice exercises.
items.body: I had 5 years in automotive manufacturing and the UPES Operations MBA gave me the management credentials for a production manager role. UPES alumni network at Haridwar and Rishikesh industrial estates was genuinely helpful.

items.name: Manish Sharma
items.liked: Production Planning and Inventory Control gave strong MRP grounding. Service Operations was well-structured for my role at a Delhi NCR logistics company.
items.disliked: For pan-India operations brand recognition, NMIMS carries stronger corporate recognition. UPES is strongest in energy and manufacturing sectors.
items.body: Good programme for North India manufacturing and logistics careers. NIRF #36 in Management was a credibility anchor. For energy sector operations, UPES has genuine competitive advantages over peer online MBAs.

items.name: Deepak Rawat
items.liked: Lean Manufacturing and Logistics subjects were strong. Technology Management gave useful digital transformation frameworks.
items.disliked: Some faculty for elective subjects lacked hands-on manufacturing industry experience. Case studies could be more India-manufacturing specific.
items.body: Solid operations credential at Rs 1,75,000. Best for North India manufacturing and energy operations careers. For South India IT services operations roles, LPU or JAIN brands may be better recognised.

faqs.question: What is the total fee for UPES Online MBA Operations Management?
faqs.answer: The total program fee is Rs 1,75,000 across 2 years. Year 1 is Rs 95,000 and Year 2 is Rs 80,000. Zero-cost EMI is available at Rs 7,292 per month for 24 months. Reconfirm current fees with our counsellor before payment.

faqs.question: What scholarships does UPES offer for Online MBA?
faqs.answer: UPES offers Academic/Work Experience scholarship (10% for 3+ years, 15% for 5+ years, 20% for 10+ years experience with 55%+ graduation), Alumni scholarship (20%), and Vijay Sports scholarship (35% to 100%). Eligibility confirmed at admission.

faqs.question: Is UPES Operations MBA suitable for energy sector manufacturing roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. UPES's 20+ year legacy in energy, petroleum, and engineering education gives operations graduates direct access to alumni networks at ONGC, NTPC, BHEL, and industrial companies. Production Planning, Lean Manufacturing, and Technology Management directly apply to energy sector plant and manufacturing operations roles.

faqs.question: What is Product Lifecycle Management in UPES Operations MBA?
faqs.answer: Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) in Sem 3 covers PLM software management, cross-functional design-to-delivery operations, and product development stage management. It is uncommon as a dedicated MBA subject in online programmes at this fee range and is directly applicable to manufacturing, automotive, and technology product operations roles.

faqs.question: How does UPES Operations Management compare to NMIMS Operations?
faqs.answer: NMIMS Operations combines data science with operations and carries strong Mumbai corporate brand recognition. UPES Operations Management includes PLM and Technology Management as distinct subjects and carries NIRF #36 Management ranking (stronger than NMIMS's management category standing). For North India manufacturing and energy sector operations, UPES has stronger alumni network advantages.

chooseIf: You want a NAAC A, NIRF #36 Management operations MBA with Lean Manufacturing, PLM, and TQM at Rs 1,75,000.
You are targeting production manager, operations manager, or quality manager roles at manufacturing, automotive, or energy sector companies.
You want zero-cost EMI at Rs 7,292/month and WES-recognised degree credentials.
consider: If analytics-integrated operations management is the goal, NMIMS Online MBA Operations and Data Sciences Management carries strong Mumbai brand with quantitative analytics depth.
If logistics and supply chain is the primary focus rather than manufacturing operations, UPES's own Logistics and SCM specialisation or LPU Logistics and SCM cover more SCM-specific subjects.
If pan-India operations brand recognition is the priority, Amity Online MBA Operations Management offers stronger all-India recognition.
